hook_text: I gave a presentation to 12 Oman HR directors. Only 2 were tracking their own data.
body:
Last October. An HR networking event in Muscat.

I asked the room a simple question: "How many of you know your company's average time-to-hire?"

2 hands out of 12.

Second question: "Average sick leave days per employee per year?"

1 hand.

Third: "WPS first-pass approval rate?"

Zero hands.

These weren't bad HR professionals. Several had 15+ years of experience.

But they were operating on intuition, not data.

The reason: their tools don't produce metrics. Excel tracks transactions, not trends.

HR in Oman is shifting from administrative to strategic — but strategic decisions require data.

You cannot argue for headcount investment without attrition data.
You cannot defend payroll process changes without compliance metrics.
You cannot benchmark benefits without knowing your cost per employee.

The HR leaders who will have a seat at the leadership table in 2027 are the ones building their data practices now.

---

What's one HR metric you wish you could track — but can't with your current tools?
rejected_reason: pre-PR#12 backlog purge — queue hygiene 2026-07-15 (regenerate via corrected pipeline)
